subject: Statistics
title: Pitch Framing in Baseball
description: A quick win: understand Pitch Framing in Baseball.

hook.text: Two catchers receive the same pitch. One gets a strike call, the other a ball. The difference is worth millions.

buildup.text: Pitch framing is a catcher's ability to receive pitches in a way that makes umpires more likely to call strikes.

discovery.text: The best framers add 20+ extra strikes per season. Each extra strike is worth about 0.13 runs.

twist.text: Until tracking data, framing was invisible. Catchers got credit for batting, not for the subtle art of receiving.

climax.text: Pitch framing analytics changed catcher valuations overnight. Some bench players became stars in the data.

punchline.text: A subtle hand movement worth millions. Data revealed the invisible skill.

quiz.question: What is pitch framing in baseball?
options: A catcher's technique of receiving pitches to increase the chance of a strike call
A scoring system for umpire accuracy
